reasoning: A wins on craft and originality: the Bet Slip (a prediction market that grades the adults and makes low expectations "a losing position with a public scoreboard") and the False-Color Scan (a wordless emotional heat map for the child) are genuinely novel, and every idea is made concrete with sample lines and earned metaphors (aviation black box, seed-packet husbandry, sculptor's negative space). B is close behind and arguably the most evenly excellent set, with sharply distinct axes (Wake, Friction Log, Portfolio of Wrongness, Constellation, Seismograph) and crisp borrowed frames, but it lands a notch below A on peak originality and vividness. C is strong, with the Confidence Map (calibration, "confidently wrong is the worst territory") as a standout, yet its Wake and Artifact overlap with ideas other slots render more boldly and its prose is less alive. D ranks last: it opens by naming its framework and a literal file path, which breaks the artifact frame and reads as scaffolding, and several of its five (time capsule, evidence exhibition, institution-graded card) are weaker restatements of concepts A, B, and C execute with more nerve, leaving only the Debt and Dividend Ledger as a fresh contribution.
